summary: Comprehensive testing of Today Task application completed. Backend API tests: 15 passed, 1 failed (brute force protection bug), 2 errors (test fixture collision). Frontend UI tests: All features working correctly including auth, tasks CRUD, settings, themes, subscription modal, and locked/unlocked features for free/premium users.

critical.issue: Brute force protection causes 500 Internal Server Error after lockout. TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes in check_brute_force function at line 76
critical.fix: Change 'datetime.now(timezone.utc) < lockout_until' to compare timezone-aware datetimes. The lockout_until stored in MongoDB may be naive. Use: 'lockout_until.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if lockout_until.tzinfo is None else lockout_until'

passed_tests: UI: Task creation with date/time picker
UI: Theme switching (Claro, Oscuro, Azul, Verde)
UI: Locked fonts for free user
UI: Locked alarms for free user
UI: Unlock buttons open subscription modal
UI: Subscription modal from Pro button
UI: Notification dropdown with all options
UI: Logout redirects to auth page
UI: Premium user has unlocked fonts (3)
UI: Premium user has unlocked alarms (4)
UI: No Pro button for premium user

failed_tests.test: Brute force lockout after 5 failed attempts
failed_tests.reason: Returns 500 instead of 429 due to datetime comparison bug

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Brute force protection datetime comparison bug in /app/backend/server.py line 76. The lockout_until datetime from MongoDB is timezone-naive but being compared with timezone-aware datetime.now(timezone.utc). Fix: ensure both datetimes are timezone-aware before comparison.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL BUG: server.py line 76 - datetime comparison fails between naive and aware datetimes causing 500 error on brute force lockout check
seed_data_creation: Admin user seeded on startup with premium status
context_for_next_testing_agent: Brute force protection has a datetime bug that causes 500 errors. Clear login_attempts collection before testing admin login if lockout occurred. All other features working correctly. Stripe integration uses test key and works.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In check_brute_force function (line 76), lockout_until datetime stored in MongoDB is timezone-naive, but it's being compared with datetime.now(timezone.utc) which is timezone-aware. Python raises TypeError when comparing naive and aware datetimes.
reproduction_steps: 1. Make 5 failed login attempts with wrong password
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Fix the datetime comparison by ensuring both datetimes are timezone-aware. Either store timezone-aware datetime in MongoDB or convert the retrieved datetime to timezone-aware before comparison.
